verses.text: He foretells His crucifixion in two days; the priests plot to take Him by stealth. Set in motion. Even the malice of men serves the appointed hour.
He foretells His crucifixion in two days; the priests plot to take Him by stealth. Set in motion. Even the malice of men serves the appointed hour.
He foretells His crucifixion in two days; the priests plot to take Him by stealth. Set in motion. Even the malice of men serves the appointed hour.
He foretells His crucifixion in two days; the priests plot to take Him by stealth. Set in motion. Even the malice of men serves the appointed hour.
He foretells His crucifixion in two days; the priests plot to take Him by stealth. Set in motion. Even the malice of men serves the appointed hour.
A woman pours costly ointment on His head; the disciples grumble at the waste. Misjudged love. They see extravagance where Christ sees devotion.
A woman pours costly ointment on His head; the disciples grumble at the waste. Misjudged love. They see extravagance where Christ sees devotion.
A woman pours costly ointment on His head; the disciples grumble at the waste. Misjudged love. They see extravagance where Christ sees devotion.
A woman pours costly ointment on His head; the disciples grumble at the waste. Misjudged love. They see extravagance where Christ sees devotion.
He says she has done it 'for my burial'; wherever the gospel is preached, this shall be told for her memorial. Honoured love. Christ remembers the kindness others despise.
He says she has done it 'for my burial'; wherever the gospel is preached, this shall be told for her memorial. Honoured love. Christ remembers the kindness others despise.
He says she has done it 'for my burial'; wherever the gospel is preached, this shall be told for her memorial. Honoured love. Christ remembers the kindness others despise.
He says she has done it 'for my burial'; wherever the gospel is preached, this shall be told for her memorial. Honoured love. Christ remembers the kindness others despise.
Judas goes to the priests; for thirty pieces of silver he seeks to betray Him. Sold for silver. The Lord of glory is bargained away at a slave's price.
Judas goes to the priests; for thirty pieces of silver he seeks to betray Him. Sold for silver. The Lord of glory is bargained away at a slave's price.
Judas goes to the priests; for thirty pieces of silver he seeks to betray Him. Sold for silver. The Lord of glory is bargained away at a slave's price.
The disciples prepare the passover where He appointed. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter for the holy meal.
The disciples prepare the passover where He appointed. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter for the holy meal.
The disciples prepare the passover where He appointed. Obedient preparation. They follow His word to the letter for the holy meal.
As they eat He says, 'One of you shall betray me'; sorrowful, each asks, 'Lord, is it I?' Searching word. The warning makes each man examine his own heart.
As they eat He says, 'One of you shall betray me'; sorrowful, each asks, 'Lord, is it I?' Searching word. The warning makes each man examine his own heart.
As they eat He says, 'One of you shall betray me'; sorrowful, each asks, 'Lord, is it I?' Searching word. The warning makes each man examine his own heart.
He that dips with me shall betray me; Judas asks, 'Master, is it I?' 'Thou hast said.' Unmasked. The traitor is named even as he feigns innocence.
He that dips with me shall betray me; Judas asks, 'Master, is it I?' 'Thou hast said.' Unmasked. The traitor is named even as he feigns innocence.
He that dips with me shall betray me; Judas asks, 'Master, is it I?' 'Thou hast said.' Unmasked. The traitor is named even as he feigns innocence.
He breaks bread, 'This is my body,' and gives the cup, 'this is my blood... shed for many for the remission of sins.' The new covenant. His death, sealed in the supper, brings pardon.
He breaks bread, 'This is my body,' and gives the cup, 'this is my blood... shed for many for the remission of sins.' The new covenant. His death, sealed in the supper, brings pardon.
He breaks bread, 'This is my body,' and gives the cup, 'this is my blood... shed for many for the remission of sins.' The new covenant. His death, sealed in the supper, brings pardon.
He will drink it new in the Father's kingdom; they sing a hymn and go out. Hope beyond death. Even on the eve of the cross He looks to the joy ahead.
He will drink it new in the Father's kingdom; they sing a hymn and go out. Hope beyond death. Even on the eve of the cross He looks to the joy ahead.
All shall be offended; Peter vows he never will, but is told, 'thou shalt deny me thrice.' Self-confidence. Peter trusts his own strength and will fall.
All shall be offended; Peter vows he never will, but is told, 'thou shalt deny me thrice.' Self-confidence. Peter trusts his own strength and will fall.
All shall be offended; Peter vows he never will, but is told, 'thou shalt deny me thrice.' Self-confidence. Peter trusts his own strength and will fall.
All shall be offended; Peter vows he never will, but is told, 'thou shalt deny me thrice.' Self-confidence. Peter trusts his own strength and will fall.
All shall be offended; Peter vows he never will, but is told, 'thou shalt deny me thrice.' Self-confidence. Peter trusts his own strength and will fall.
In Gethsemane His soul is 'exceeding sorrowful, even unto death'; He bids them watch with Him. Deep anguish. The Saviour shrinks under the weight of what He must bear.
In Gethsemane His soul is 'exceeding sorrowful, even unto death'; He bids them watch with Him. Deep anguish. The Saviour shrinks under the weight of what He must bear.
In Gethsemane His soul is 'exceeding sorrowful, even unto death'; He bids them watch with Him. Deep anguish. The Saviour shrinks under the weight of what He must bear.
He prays, 'let this cup pass... nevertheless not as I will, but as thou wilt'; 'the spirit... is willing, but the flesh is weak.' Submission. He yields His will wholly to the Father's.
He prays, 'let this cup pass... nevertheless not as I will, but as thou wilt'; 'the spirit... is willing, but the flesh is weak.' Submission. He yields His will wholly to the Father's.
He prays, 'let this cup pass... nevertheless not as I will, but as thou wilt'; 'the spirit... is willing, but the flesh is weak.' Submission. He yields His will wholly to the Father's.
He prays again, 'Thy will be done,' and finds the disciples asleep. Lonely struggle. He bears His agony while His friends slumber.
He prays again, 'Thy will be done,' and finds the disciples asleep. Lonely struggle. He bears His agony while His friends slumber.
He prays again, 'Thy will be done,' and finds the disciples asleep. Lonely struggle. He bears His agony while His friends slumber.
Sleep on now; the hour is at hand; rise, the betrayer comes. The hour arrives. He goes calmly to meet His appointed suffering.
Sleep on now; the hour is at hand; rise, the betrayer comes. The hour arrives. He goes calmly to meet His appointed suffering.
Judas comes with a multitude and a kiss; 'Friend, wherefore art thou come?' Betrayed with affection. The sign of love becomes the mark of treachery.
Judas comes with a multitude and a kiss; 'Friend, wherefore art thou come?' Betrayed with affection. The sign of love becomes the mark of treachery.
Judas comes with a multitude and a kiss; 'Friend, wherefore art thou come?' Betrayed with affection. The sign of love becomes the mark of treachery.
Judas comes with a multitude and a kiss; 'Friend, wherefore art thou come?' Betrayed with affection. The sign of love becomes the mark of treachery.
One draws a sword; Jesus bids him sheathe it; could He not pray for legions of angels? Willing surrender. He could be rescued, but He chooses the cross.
One draws a sword; Jesus bids him sheathe it; could He not pray for legions of angels? Willing surrender. He could be rescued, but He chooses the cross.
One draws a sword; Jesus bids him sheathe it; could He not pray for legions of angels? Willing surrender. He could be rescued, but He chooses the cross.
One draws a sword; Jesus bids him sheathe it; could He not pray for legions of angels? Willing surrender. He could be rescued, but He chooses the cross.
Am I a thief, that ye come with swords? all is done that the scriptures be fulfilled; the disciples flee. Scripture fulfilled. Even His arrest accomplishes God's written word.
Am I a thief, that ye come with swords? all is done that the scriptures be fulfilled; the disciples flee. Scripture fulfilled. Even His arrest accomplishes God's written word.
Before Caiaphas the council seeks false witness but finds none agreeing. Rigged trial. They hunt for a charge against the innocent.
Before Caiaphas the council seeks false witness but finds none agreeing. Rigged trial. They hunt for a charge against the innocent.
Before Caiaphas the council seeks false witness but finds none agreeing. Rigged trial. They hunt for a charge against the innocent.
Before Caiaphas the council seeks false witness but finds none agreeing. Rigged trial. They hunt for a charge against the innocent.
Two twist His words about the temple; the high priest demands an answer, but He holds His peace. Silent Lamb. He answers not the slanders heaped upon Him.
Two twist His words about the temple; the high priest demands an answer, but He holds His peace. Silent Lamb. He answers not the slanders heaped upon Him.
Two twist His words about the temple; the high priest demands an answer, but He holds His peace. Silent Lamb. He answers not the slanders heaped upon Him.
Adjured, He owns He is the Christ, to be seen coming in glory; they cry blasphemy and condemn Him. Truth confessed. He seals His own sentence by confessing who He is.
Adjured, He owns He is the Christ, to be seen coming in glory; they cry blasphemy and condemn Him. Truth confessed. He seals His own sentence by confessing who He is.
Adjured, He owns He is the Christ, to be seen coming in glory; they cry blasphemy and condemn Him. Truth confessed. He seals His own sentence by confessing who He is.
They spit on Him, buffet Him, and mock, 'Prophesy... who smote thee?' Shameful abuse. The Lord of glory endures the mockery of men.
They spit on Him, buffet Him, and mock, 'Prophesy... who smote thee?' Shameful abuse. The Lord of glory endures the mockery of men.
In the courtyard Peter is charged with knowing Jesus; he denies it twice. Sliding fall. Step by step the boaster sinks into denial.
In the courtyard Peter is charged with knowing Jesus; he denies it twice. Sliding fall. Step by step the boaster sinks into denial.
In the courtyard Peter is charged with knowing Jesus; he denies it twice. Sliding fall. Step by step the boaster sinks into denial.
In the courtyard Peter is charged with knowing Jesus; he denies it twice. Sliding fall. Step by step the boaster sinks into denial.
His speech betrays him; he curses and swears he knows not the man; the cock crows, and he weeps bitterly. Bitter tears. The fall is grievous, but the repentance is real.
His speech betrays him; he curses and swears he knows not the man; the cock crows, and he weeps bitterly. Bitter tears. The fall is grievous, but the repentance is real.
